rulings.Case Title: LEILA YEE et al VS. TRALEE MANAGEMENT LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Dismissing X-Complaint Causes Of Action Against Tralee Management, Llc And Mark Brown
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 21, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANTS TRALEE MANAGEMENT, LLC, and, MARK BROWN Motion for Order Dismissing Cross-Complaint Causes of Action Against Tralee Management, LLC and Mark Brown. Motion is granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LEILA YEE et al VS. TRALEE MANAGEMENT LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 21, 2010, line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANTS MWAMBA WAMWAMBA, and, MAUREEN KENNEY Motion for Leave to File a Second Amended Cross Complaint. Motion denied. The first three causes of action are barred by CCP section 877.6, and the fourth cause of action fails to state a claim of express indemnity against Brown, as cross-complaint fails to allege that Brown as a party to the residential listing agreement. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD BOWLING JR. VS. ADVERTISING DISPLAY SYSTEMS I, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 21, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF RICHARD BOWLING Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement. Motion granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's request for judgment for $37,650.93 granted against ADS only. CCP section 664.6 does not apply to individual managers because they were never "parties" to this action. Defendant fails to show that it is proper to name individual managers as judgment debtors per CCP section 187. Plaintiff fails to present sufficient alter ego evidence. Motion to amend denied because motion not properly calendared. Plaintiff's remedy may be to commence a new action for breach of the guarantees. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: REGINALD SMITH VS. FINE LINE CONSTRUCTION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 21, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT J&R THOMPSON, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. (Court treats plaintiff's opposition as opposition to both demurrer and motion to strike.) Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to support each cause of action and specify to which defendants they apply. Plaintiff must plead legally recognized causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: REGINALD SMITH VS. FINE LINE CONSTRUCTION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 21, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT J&R THOMPSON, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike denied as not properly noticed and addressed. Points and authorities improperly reference the special motion to strike and fail to adequately develop arguments. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LEO SANDERS JR VS. DAVID DENG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 21, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANTS DAVID DENG, and, ANGELINA DENG DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with 20 days leave to amend. rulings.Case Title: YOLANDA MIANI, ET AL VS. LABIB MAJID BARANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 21, 2010, line 12, DEFENDANT COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to 4th and 6th causes of action as arguments are based on the merits and are not proper for demurrer. A split of authority exists as to whether unjust enrichment exists as a separate cause of action. Overruled as to 10th cause of action. Demurerr sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to remaining causes of action. First cause of action (breach of contract) contains uncertain and inconsistent allegations regarding the oral contract. The second agreement must state the terms with respect to CLTC and state whether written or oral. The 2nd cause of action (breach of implied covenant) is dependent upon 1st cause of action. Third cause of action (B&P Code section 17200) must distinquish the defendants and allege conduct specific to each. As to 5th cause of action (intentional mispresentation) and 7th cause of action (fraudulent concealment) plaintiff must plead with specificity: who, what, when, and by whom. =(302/CWW)
